sections.paragraph: f. cup, calix, calyx, both of a flower, κάλυξ (Arab. قُبْعَةُ; compare כֹּובַע and קֹובַע), and also for drinking from, κύλιξ; hence, Isa 51:17, 22 Isa 51:22, קֻבַּעַת כֹּוס “the chalice of the cup.” Abulwalid understands the froth and dregs of the cup (from the idea of covering), but the explanation already given is the better.
